mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals an impending economic contraction, forcing analysts to downgrade corporate earnings estimates and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal fails to predict earnings downgrades within 60 days.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing construction and retail sales expenditure indicates robust consumer demand, which expands corporate revenue pipelines and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of observations, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in industrial production following a supply disruption normalizes input availability, reduces commodity pass-through costs, and lifts corporate profit margins, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain flat or negative when INDPRO accelerates, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for oil price movements over the same 5-day window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, financial conditions tighten sufficiently to restrict corporate borrowing and consumer credit, suppressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns are positive in the majority of instances where FEDFUNDS crosses 5.0%, or the threshold signal shows no incremental predictive power over a linear FEDFUNDS specification.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with moderating consumer price inflation confirms a soft-landing scenario, reducing recession risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or the cross-product term fails to improve model fit compared to single-series baselines over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real gross domestic product directly expands the aggregate revenue base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically translating into positive equity returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% annualized, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over any rolling 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently low unemployment alongside rising inflation expectations forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when UNRATE stays below 4.0% and CPIAUCSL accelerates, or the hypothesis fails to predict multiple compression in a forward-looking valuation model.
